title: Ta Prohm – Exploring the “Tomb Raider Temple” and Its Tree-Choked Ruins - Expat Life Cambodia
highlights:
where colossal strangler fig and silk-cotton trees wrap themselves around  ...    ### Abandonment and Preservation Philosophy After the decline of the Khmer Empire in the 15th century, Ta Prohm was gradually abandoned. When French conservationists began restoring Angkor in the 19th and 20th centuries, they made a deliberate decision: Ta Prohm would remain largely as it was found. This contrasts with highly restored sites such as Angkor Wat. The result is an immersive, jungle-overgrown  ...    ### Ta Prohm Trees and Roots

What sets Ta Prohm apart are its legendary trees: - Tetrameles nudiflora (silk-cotton trees)
